page_content:
UNKNOWN incident: Payments service timing out
affected: payments
description: Users are reporting that checkout is hanging indefinitely. The payments page just spins and never completes. We think the database might be overloaded because CPU is at 80% on the payments pod. No errors visible in the UI.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Checkout flow broken – payments not going through
affected: payments
description: Multiple customers emailed saying their cards are being charged but orders aren't placed. Suspect a race condition in the order creation logic introduced in last week's deploy. Engineering is investigating the new checkout refactor.
resolution: open

P3 incident: [P1] Stripe API returning 503 – payment processing down
affected: payments
description: Confirmed: Stripe status page shows degraded performance on their payments API since 11:58 UTC. Our circuit breaker has opened. All payment requests are being queued. ETA from Stripe is 30–45 minutes. No action needed on our infra side.
resolution: Stripe resolved their incident at 12:51 UTC. Circuit breaker reset automatically. Payment queue drained within 4 minutes. No data loss. Post-mortem scheduled.

P3 incident: Payment gateway down – customers can't complete purchases
affected: payments
description: High volume of failed transactions since roughly midday. Our on-call engineer initially suspected the Redis session cache because there was a cache miss spike around the same time, but that may be a red herring. Still investigating root cause.
resolution: Root cause was Stripe-side. Redis cache miss spike was unrelated — caused by a separate routine cache eviction job that ran at noon.

UNKNOWN incident: Users being logged out randomly
affected: auth
description: Support queue blowing up with users saying they're being kicked out mid-session. Happens across browsers and devices. Someone on the team thinks the load balancer is dropping sticky sessions, but we haven't confirmed this yet.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: JWT token validation failing intermittently
affected: auth
description: Auth service logs show 'signature mismatch' errors on roughly 12% of token validations. This started after the secret rotation at 09:15 UTC. Old tokens signed with the previous secret are being rejected because we forgot to keep the old key in the validation chain during the rotation window.
resolution: Added previous signing key back to the JWKS endpoint. Deployed hotfix v2.14.1. Affected users need to log in again — ~3,400 sessions invalidated. Added key rotation runbook to prevent recurrence.

P1 incident: Login broken after maintenance window
affected: auth
description: Since the 09:00 maintenance, a subset of users can't log in. Error message says 'Invalid credentials' even though passwords are correct. Initially thought it was a password hashing algorithm change, spent 2 hours on that. Actual cause still unknown.
resolution: Duplicate of TKT-auth_jwt. Root cause was secret rotation mismatch, not password hashing.

P1 incident: Orders service throwing 500 errors
affected: orders
description: Order creation endpoint returning HTTP 500 since 14:32. Initial theory is that the recent traffic spike caused the service to run out of memory — the pod was restarted twice in the last hour. Memory graphs look elevated.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: [CRITICAL] Orders DB disk quota exceeded
affected: orders
description: Postgres data volume on orders-db-primary hit 100% capacity at 14:31 UTC. Writes are failing, causing cascading 500s from the orders service. The WAL archive directory has not been pruned in 47 days due to a misconfigured cron job. Immediate action: extend volume or prune WAL.
resolution: Pruned WAL archive (freed 180GB). Extended EBS volume to 500GB. Restarted orders-db-primary. Orders service recovered at 15:02 UTC. Fixed cron job schedule. Added disk alerting at 75% and 90% thresholds.

P3 incident: Can't place orders – getting server error
affected: orders
description: Customers reporting 'Something went wrong' on checkout. This is the second time this week orders have gone down. We think the culprit might be the new inventory-check microservice that was released Monday — it's making synchronous calls and could be blocking the thread pool.
resolution: Actual root cause was disk quota on orders-db. The inventory-check service was functioning normally.

UNKNOWN incident: API gateway returning 502 for all requests
affected: gateway
description: 100% of API calls returning 502 Bad Gateway starting 14:03. Nginx error logs show upstream connection refused. The DevOps team is checking if a bad deployment rolled out to the gateway — last deploy was 3 hours ago though.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Circuit breaker open on gateway – payments upstream down
affected: gateway
description: Gateway circuit breaker tripped open for the payments-service upstream at 14:01 UTC after consecutive 503 responses. This is expected behavior. Gateway is correctly returning 503 with Retry-After header. No gateway config change needed — waiting on payments recovery.
resolution: Circuit breaker reset at 13:05 UTC once Stripe recovered and payments-service returned healthy responses. Gateway fully operational.

UNKNOWN incident: All endpoints down – site appears to be offline
affected: gateway
description: Monitoring alert fired: external uptime check failing from 3 regions. Customers tweeting about the site being down. The CEO has been notified. Our DNS might have been misconfigured — someone changed Route53 settings this morning. Checking with the infra team.
resolution: DNS was fine. Outage caused by gateway circuit breaker opening due to upstream payments failure. Route53 changes were unrelated (added a new subdomain). Communications team sent status page update.

P3 incident: Order events not being processed
affected: orders
description: The order-fulfillment consumer has a growing lag on the orders.created topic — currently 84,000 unprocessed messages. Started accumulating around 14:35. Consumer pods appear healthy but throughput dropped to near zero. Possibly a poison-pill message blocking the partition.
resolution: Identified a malformed order event (missing required 'items' field) causing deserialization exception in the consumer. Added dead-letter queue routing for unparseable messages. Consumer lag cleared within 18 minutes after fix.

UNKNOWN incident: Fulfillment emails not sending
affected: orders
description: Customers aren't receiving order confirmation emails after purchase. This started around 2:30pm. The email service itself is healthy according to its own dashboard. We think SendGrid might be throttling us — we did send a large batch campaign this morning.
resolution: Root cause was Kafka consumer lag, not SendGrid. Emails were queued but not consumed. All queued emails delivered once consumer recovered.

UNKNOWN incident: Redis OOM – cache cluster out of memory
affected: auth
description: Redis maxmemory limit hit on the session cache cluster. Eviction policy set to noeviction, so new writes are failing with OOM errors. Auth service is logging thousands of cache write failures per minute. Session data is falling through to the database, causing query load spike.
resolution: Changed eviction policy to allkeys-lru temporarily. Increased Redis maxmemory from 4GB to 8GB. Scheduled capacity planning review.

UNKNOWN incident: High latency on product search – users complaining
affected: gateway
description: P95 latency on /api/v1/search jumped from 120ms to 4200ms at around 10:45. Elasticsearch cluster shows elevated CPU. We rolled back the search ranking model deployed at 10:30 but latency didn't improve, so that probably wasn't it.
resolution: Root cause: a new analytics pipeline started a full-index rebuild job at 10:40, saturating Elasticsearch I/O. Paused the rebuild, latency normalized. Rebuild rescheduled for off-peak hours.

UNKNOWN incident: Payment refunds being double-processed
affected: payments
description: Refund endpoint lacks idempotency key validation. When Stripe's webhook retries (3x), our handler processes the refund each time. Discovered when a customer received 3x their refund amount. Estimated financial impact: ~$1,200 across 4 customers.
resolution: Added idempotency key check using Stripe's charge ID as the deduplication key. Deployed fix. Manual reversal initiated for 4 affected customers. Stripe credited back overage.

P2 incident: TLS cert expiring in 3 days – urgent renewal needed
affected: gateway
description: Automated cert renewal via Certbot failed silently last week. The wildcard cert for *.api.example.com expires 2024-06-17. If not renewed, all HTTPS traffic will fail. The failure was due to a DNS challenge timeout — we changed DNS providers and forgot to update Certbot config.
resolution: Manually renewed cert via new DNS provider API. Updated Certbot config. Set up monitoring alert for cert expiry < 14 days. Added cert renewal to monthly ops checklist.

UNKNOWN incident: Users seeing other users' order history
affected: orders
description: Critical privacy bug. Three reports of users seeing a different account's order list on the /orders/history endpoint. This is almost certainly a caching bug — the CDN or application cache may be keying responses without user context. Immediate investigation required.
resolution: Confirmed CDN cache misconfiguration: Vary header was missing, causing authenticated responses to be served from shared cache. Purged CDN cache, added Vary: Authorization, Cache-Control: private headers. Affected users notified. Security review underway.

UNKNOWN incident: Auth service memory leak – pod restarting every 6 hours
affected: auth
description: auth-service pod hits memory limit and gets OOMKilled every ~6 hours. Heap dumps show accumulation of open HTTP client connections that aren't being released. Suspected the connection pool isn't being closed properly after JWT validation calls to the user-service.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Spike in 401s – customers locked out
affected: auth
description: 401 error rate went from 0.3% to 18% at 09:15. Support is overwhelmed. We believe this is a frontend bug — the React app might not be sending the Authorization header correctly after the UI deploy this morning. Frontend team is checking.
resolution: Duplicate investigation with auth_jwt cluster. Root cause was server-side JWT key rotation, not a frontend bug. Frontend deploy was coincidental.

UNKNOWN incident: Payments pod CrashLoopBackOff
affected: payments
description: payments-service pod in CrashLoopBackOff since 14:05. Logs show 'Fatal: cannot connect to postgres on startup' — but the DB host/port config hasn't changed. Someone may have accidentally deleted the kubernetes secret containing the DB credentials during the earlier infra work.
resolution: Confirmed: k8s secret 'payments-db-credentials' was deleted at 13:58 during routine secret rotation. Secret recreated from vault backup. Pod recovered at 14:11. Added RBAC restriction on secret deletion in production namespace.

P1 incident: Slow dashboard load times reported by enterprise customers
affected: orders
description: Three enterprise accounts opened support tickets about slow dashboard loads (8–15 seconds). Normal load time is ~1.2 seconds. These accounts have large datasets. We suspect it might be a missing index on the orders table after last week's migration, or possibly just their network connection.
resolution: Found missing composite index on (customer_id, created_at) that was dropped during migration. Added index — query time dropped from 12s to 180ms. Network was not the issue.

UNKNOWN incident: Webhook delivery failures to merchant integrations
affected: orders
description: Outbound webhooks for the orders.completed event failing with connection timeouts to 3 merchant endpoints. Our webhook worker is retrying with exponential backoff. Two of the merchants confirmed their servers are fine — we may have an egress network policy blocking outbound port 443 to external IPs.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Incorrect tax calculation on European orders
affected: payments
description: EU customers being charged wrong VAT rates since the VAT table update deployed on June 12th. Austria is being taxed at 19% (Germany rate) instead of 20%. Approximately 340 orders affected. This might be a data entry error in the tax config CSV, or possibly a country-code mapping bug.
resolution: Confirmed data entry error: Austria (AT) was mapped to DE in the tax config CSV. Corrected mapping, redeployed config. Issued credit notes for 340 affected orders.

UNKNOWN incident: Mobile app can't reach API – 'Network Error' on login
affected: gateway
description: iOS app users on version 3.2.1 getting 'Network Error' on login since this morning. Android unaffected. Backend team checked and all endpoints are healthy. This is almost certainly a mobile-side bug — maybe App Transport Security blocking non-HTTPS, or a base URL misconfiguration in the release build.
resolution: App build 3.2.1 had incorrect API base URL pointing to staging environment (api-staging.example.com) instead of production. Fix released in 3.2.2 via hotfix. Users prompted to update.

UNKNOWN incident: Order status stuck on 'Processing' for 2+ hours
affected: orders
description: About 200 orders created between 14:30 and 15:00 are stuck in 'Processing' state. The fulfillment pipeline reads from the Kafka topic orders.created but consumer lag was high during the incident window. These orders may have been lost. Need to check if they need to be manually reprocessed.
resolution: Orders were not lost — they were queued in Kafka. After consumer lag cleared, all 200 orders were processed successfully. Status updated automatically. No manual intervention needed.

UNKNOWN incident: Suspicious spike in password reset requests
affected: auth
description: Password reset endpoint received 12,000 requests in a 10-minute window at 11:20 UTC, compared to a baseline of ~40/hour. Could be a credential stuffing attack or a bot trying to enumerate valid email addresses. Rate limiting on this endpoint is set to 100/min globally, which is clearly insufficient.
resolution: Confirmed bot activity from 3 IP ranges. Blocked IPs via WAF. Reduced rate limit to 5 requests/min per IP on the reset endpoint. Added CAPTCHA to reset flow. No evidence of successful account takeovers.

UNKNOWN incident: Grafana shows payments latency at 0 – metrics missing
affected: payments
description: Payments latency dashboard has been showing flat zero for the past 2 hours. Either the payment service has magically zero latency, or — more likely — the Prometheus scrape job stopped working. Could also be a broken metric name after the recent instrumentation refactor.
resolution: Metric name changed from payment_request_duration_ms to payment_request_duration_seconds in the refactor but the Grafana query wasn't updated. Fixed dashboard query. Added metric name validation to CI pipeline.

P1 incident: 500 error on /api/v1/orders/export for large datasets
affected: orders
description: The CSV export feature crashes when exporting more than ~10,000 orders. Error in logs: 'Worker process exited with OOM'. The export endpoint loads all rows into memory before writing the CSV. This has probably always been broken for large accounts — we just didn't have any until now.
resolution: open

P1 incident: Payments service not starting after config change
affected: payments
description: After updating the payments service environment variables to point to the new Postgres replica, the service refuses to start. Logs show 'SSL required' error. The new replica has SSL enforced but our connection string doesn't include sslmode=require. Thought the replica would have the same config as primary.
resolution: Added sslmode=require to connection string. Service started successfully. Noted that replica SSL config differs from primary — added this to the infra runbook.

UNKNOWN incident: Gateway health check failing – marked unhealthy by load balancer
affected: gateway
description: ALB health check on /health endpoint returning 200 but with a 3.2 second response time, causing the ALB to mark instances as unhealthy and deregister them. The /health endpoint is doing a deep check including a DB query which is timing out under load. Shallow health check would have avoided this.
resolution: Changed /health to return immediately (shallow check). Added separate /health/deep endpoint for internal monitoring only. Instances re-registered within 2 minutes.

P3 incident: New user signup failing silently – no error shown
affected: auth
description: Signups appearing to succeed on the frontend (green success toast shown) but users not receiving welcome email and accounts not being created. The signup API is returning 200 even when the downstream user creation fails. This masks the error from users and from our monitoring.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Intermittent 504 on checkout – happening maybe 5% of requests
affected: gateway
description: Gateway logs show occasional 504 timeouts on POST /checkout. Happens under high load. Upstream payments service is healthy. We initially blamed a noisy neighbor on the same node, but after moving the pod, the issue persisted. May be a connection pool saturation issue.
resolution: Found that the payments-service had a max concurrent connections limit of 50 in its internal HTTP server config, being exceeded during peak traffic. Increased to 500 and added connection pool metrics to dashboards.

UNKNOWN incident: [SEC] API keys visible in error responses
affected: payments
description: Security researcher reported that when certain malformed requests are sent to /api/v1/payments/validate, the error response includes internal configuration details including a partial API key. Reproduced locally. This looks like an unhandled exception leaking the config object via the default Flask error handler.
resolution: Replaced default Flask error handler with custom handler that returns generic error messages. Rotated all potentially exposed API keys. Patched in v2.17.3. Researcher acknowledged via bug bounty program.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders created with wrong currency
affected: orders
description: 17 orders placed by users in Canada show USD instead of CAD. The currency detection logic uses the browser's Accept-Language header, but these users had en-US locale despite being billed in Canada. This is a known limitation that was supposed to be addressed in Q2 but wasn't prioritized.
resolution: Switched currency detection to use billing address country rather than Accept-Language header. Issued corrected invoices to 17 affected customers.

UNKNOWN incident: Kafka broker disk at 94% – risk of topic unavailability
affected: orders
description: Kafka broker-2 disk utilization hit 94%. If it hits 100%, the broker will go offline and partitions will be unavailable. Log retention is set to 7 days but we've had 3x normal write volume this week due to the incident. No one owns the Kafka infra formally right now.
resolution: Reduced retention to 3 days temporarily. Added disk space alert at 80%. Long-term: move to tiered storage on S3. Assigned Kafka ownership to platform team.

UNKNOWN incident: Auth tokens not being invalidated on password change
affected: auth
description: When a user changes their password, existing sessions remain valid. This means if an account is compromised and the user changes their password to kick out the attacker, the attacker's token still works until natural expiry (24 hours). This is a significant security gap.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Duplicate orders being created on payment retry
affected: orders
description: When a payment times out and the user retries, a new order is created instead of resuming the existing one. Affected users end up charged once but with two orders in the system, causing fulfillment to ship twice. Estimated 8–12 duplicate shipments this week.
resolution: Added idempotency token tied to cart session ID for order creation. Existing duplicates manually resolved with customer support.

UNKNOWN incident: CPU spike on auth service every night at 02:00
affected: auth
description: Auth service CPU spikes to 95% every night at exactly 02:00 for about 8 minutes. No corresponding traffic increase. Could be a scheduled job — maybe token cleanup or session pruning. It hasn't caused an outage yet but it's concerning.
resolution: Identified a nightly cron job doing a full table scan on the sessions table to clean expired tokens, with no index on expires_at. Added index — CPU spike reduced from 95% to 12%.

UNKNOWN incident: P1 – entire platform down, all services 503
affected: gateway
description: Complete platform outage. All services returning 503. This is catastrophic. Someone may have accidentally applied a network policy that blocks all ingress. Alternatively the Kubernetes control plane might be having issues — we had a K8s upgrade last night.
resolution: Root cause: payments-service outage cascaded through gateway circuit breaker to appear as full outage. K8s and network policy were fine. Improved status page to reflect per-service health rather than single global status.

P3 incident: Search ranking returning irrelevant results after model update
affected: gateway
description: The new product search ranking model (v3.1) deployed at 10:30 is returning unrelated products as top results. 'Red shoes' is returning kitchen appliances as the first result. Rolling back to v3.0 is recommended but the ML team wants to investigate first.
resolution: Model was trained on a dataset with a labeling error — a batch of product categories was shuffled during the feature extraction pipeline. Rolled back to v3.0. Retrain scheduled with corrected dataset.

UNKNOWN incident: Occasional NaN prices showing on product listings
affected: orders
description: A handful of products showing 'NaN' as their price on the storefront. Appears to be a frontend rendering issue where a null price value from the API isn't being handled. The API is returning null for products where pricing hasn't been set up — this is valid but the UI can't handle it.
resolution: Frontend fix: added null check before rendering price, falls back to 'Price on request'. API fix: added validation to prevent products without prices from being published.

UNKNOWN incident: Rate limiting too aggressive – legitimate users blocked
affected: gateway
description: After tightening rate limits following the bot attack, legitimate enterprise API users are being throttled. The new limit of 100 req/min is too low for their integration use case. One customer is threatening to churn. We may have overcorrected.
resolution: Implemented tiered rate limits: standard (100/min), enterprise (2000/min). Enterprise customers get API key-based limits rather than IP-based. Retroactively applied to affected accounts.

UNKNOWN incident: Inventory count going negative for popular SKUs
affected: orders
description: Three products showing negative inventory counts (-4, -12, -7). This means we oversold. The inventory reservation step runs asynchronously and there's a race condition when multiple orders arrive simultaneously for the same SKU. The check-and-decrement isn't atomic.
resolution: open

P1 incident: Payments service pod running as root – security finding
affected: payments
description: Security scan flagged that the payments-service container is running as UID 0 (root). This is against our security policy and poses a privilege escalation risk if the container is compromised. The Dockerfile never set a non-root user.
resolution: Added USER 1001 to Dockerfile. Added OPA policy to reject pods running as root in production namespace. Redeployed payments-service.

UNKNOWN incident: On-call alert fatigue – noise from false positive monitors
affected: auth
description: The on-call engineer received 340 PagerDuty alerts over the weekend, 90% of which were false positives from flapping monitors. The auth service 'high latency' alert fires at > 200ms but normal P95 is 180–220ms. Thresholds were never recalibrated after the infra migration.
resolution: Audited and recalibrated all alert thresholds based on 30-day baselines. Moved low-signal alerts to warning tickets instead of PagerDuty. Reduced weekend alert volume by 73%.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders API v1 deprecated but still receiving heavy traffic
affected: orders
description: API v1 was officially deprecated 6 months ago but still handling 38% of order traffic. Several enterprise customers never migrated. If we shut it down per the original plan, it will break their integrations. The migration deadline was communicated by email only — many accounts didn't act.
resolution: open

P2 incident: Missing logs for 90-minute window during incident
affected: gateway
description: Post-incident review found that application logs from 14:00–15:30 are missing from our log aggregation system. Fluentd was likely overwhelmed and dropped log records during peak error volume. This is a significant observability gap that hampers post-mortem analysis.
resolution: Increased Fluentd buffer size and added backpressure handling. Enabled log persistence to local disk as fallback before forwarding to Loki. Added log ingestion rate to monitoring dashboards.

P1 incident: Webhook signature validation not enforced
affected: payments
description: Stripe webhooks are being processed without verifying the Stripe-Signature header. This means anyone who knows our webhook endpoint URL could send fake payment success events and trigger order fulfillment without actual payment. Found during a code review.
resolution: Added Stripe webhook signature verification using stripe.WebhookEvent.construct_event(). Verified no fraudulent events were processed historically (all payloads matched expected schema). Deployed in v2.18.0.

UNKNOWN incident: Auth service slow to respond under load test
affected: auth
description: Load test with 500 concurrent users showed auth service response time degrading to 8s at peak. The bottleneck appears to be the bcrypt hashing — cost factor is set to 14 which is very high. Reducing it should help but we're worried about security implications.
resolution: Reduced bcrypt cost factor from 14 to 12 (still secure per OWASP). Added async token validation cache. P95 latency under same load dropped to 420ms.

UNKNOWN incident: Payments service deployed to wrong environment
affected: payments
description: A developer accidentally ran the production deploy script targeting the payments-service in the staging cluster instead of prod. Production payments service is on an older version for 22 minutes. No customer impact — prod was still running, just not updated.
resolution: Correct deployment completed manually. Added environment name confirmation prompt to deploy script. Added prod/staging visual distinction to deployment dashboard.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders table query taking 45 seconds during peak hours
affected: orders
description: During lunch hour peak, some order listing queries are taking 45 seconds. EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows a sequential scan on 40M row table. The query filters on status and created_at but the index only covers status. Added to backlog as 'investigate slow queries' 3 sprints ago with no action.
resolution: Added composite index on (status, created_at DESC). Query time dropped to 8ms. Established slow query alert at >1s.

UNKNOWN incident: Gateway access logs not capturing client IPs
affected: gateway
description: All gateway access logs show 10.0.0.1 as the client IP (the load balancer internal IP) instead of the real client IP. The X-Forwarded-For header exists but the gateway isn't configured to log it. This broke our geo-analytics and makes IP-based abuse investigation impossible.
resolution: Updated nginx log format to use $http_x_forwarded_for instead of $remote_addr. Confirmed real IPs now captured. Historical logs prior to fix will show internal IP.

UNKNOWN incident: Refund amounts off by a factor of 100
affected: payments
description: Three refunds processed for amounts that were 100x too large. $12.99 refund was processed as $1299.00. The payments service stores amounts in cents internally but the refund API endpoint accepts dollars — there's a double conversion happening. Stripe blocked two of the three due to amount limit.
resolution: Fixed unit conversion bug in refund endpoint. One $1299 refund went through — recovered from Stripe. Added unit tests for currency conversion edge cases.

UNKNOWN incident: Auth service not respecting account lockout policy
affected: auth
description: Security policy requires account lockout after 5 failed login attempts. Testing shows the lockout is only triggered per-pod, not globally. With 4 auth pods running, an attacker can make 20 attempts total before lockout. The lockout state needs to be in shared Redis, not in-memory.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Memory usage growing on gateway pod – possible leak
affected: gateway
description: Gateway pod memory has been growing steadily over 5 days: 400MB → 1.2GB with no traffic increase. Prometheus shows heap growing every 6 hours. The team suspects the in-process cache for route configs isn't evicting stale entries. No reproduction in dev yet.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Orders webhook retries hammering merchant endpoint
affected: orders
description: Our webhook retry logic sends requests with exponential backoff but there's no maximum retry cap. One merchant's endpoint has been down for 3 days, and our system has made 15,000 retry attempts to it. This is generating complaints from the merchant about our system DDoSing them.
resolution: Added maximum retry cap of 72 attempts over 72 hours. After cap, webhooks moved to dead-letter queue for manual review. Added merchant notification when their endpoint fails consistently.

UNKNOWN incident: Staging environment down – blocking release
affected: orders
description: Staging has been completely down since Tuesday's deployment attempt. The deploy failed mid-way and left staging in a broken state. No one has had time to fix it. Two feature releases are blocked. Developers are testing in prod-like environments on their laptops which is not ideal.
resolution: Re-provisioned staging environment from Terraform. Deployed last known good staging build. Added deployment health check with automatic rollback for future failed deploys.

P3 incident: API response bodies being cached incorrectly
affected: gateway
description: Some authenticated API responses are being cached by Cloudflare at the edge. A user reported seeing another user's account details. After investigation, the Cache-Control and Vary headers are set correctly on most endpoints but three endpoints introduced in the last sprint are missing them.
resolution: Added Cache-Control: private, no-store and Vary: Authorization to the three affected endpoints. Purged Cloudflare edge cache. Reviewed all endpoints for correct cache headers — added linting rule to CI.

UNKNOWN incident: Users saying they're being charged but getting errors
affected: payments
description: Several users posted on our community forum saying their card was charged but they got an error page. We think this might be a frontend timeout issue — the backend processes the charge but the response doesn't reach the client in time. Or possibly the confirmation page is broken.
resolution: Payments were actually pending in queue during Stripe outage. Charges were authorized then later completed. Users received delayed confirmation emails. No double-charging occurred.

UNKNOWN incident: New orders disappearing after placement
affected: orders
description: A handful of users placed orders that show as confirmed in the UI but vanish minutes later. Could be a cache coherence issue — the order appears in cache, the user sees it, then the cache invalidates and the DB write has failed silently. Needs urgent investigation.
resolution: Orders were failing at DB write level due to disk quota exhaustion. Cache showed temporary success. Orders were not actually persisted. Customers re-notified and orders resubmitted after DB recovered.

UNKNOWN incident: OAuth2 social login not working
affected: auth
description: Sign in with Google returning error after the maintenance window. The Google OAuth callback URL is correct, so this is probably something on Google's side. We should check Google Cloud Console for any API quota issues.
resolution: Google OAuth was working fine. Issue was the JWT validation failure on our end rejecting the session token post-OAuth. Same root cause as secret rotation incident.

P1 incident: API latency through the roof since 2pm
affected: gateway
description: Dashboard showing P99 latency at 28 seconds since about 14:00. Our APM tool points to the payments service as the slowest upstream. Probably a memory issue on the payments pods — we should increase the memory limit in the deployment spec.
resolution: High latency was caused by gateway queuing requests to payments-service while circuit breaker was half-open, not a memory issue. Memory limits were not relevant.

UNKNOWN incident: GDPR deletion requests not being fully honored
affected: auth
description: A user submitted a GDPR right-to-erasure request. Our deletion job removed their record from the primary DB but missed: the analytics event store, the audit log archive, and the email marketing list export in S3. This is non-compliant and a potential regulatory issue.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Inventory service not responding – orders blocked
affected: orders
description: Inventory check is timing out for all order creation requests. The inventory service itself is healthy but it's querying a Redis cluster that was restarted for maintenance. The Redis client in inventory service isn't reconnecting automatically after the restart.
resolution: Added reconnect logic and exponential backoff to Redis client configuration. Added readiness probe to inventory service that checks Redis connectivity. Deployed fix.

UNKNOWN incident: Payment page spinning for 30+ seconds
affected: payments
description: Customer reported via live chat that checkout hangs on payment step. No error message shown. Refreshing the page loses their cart. Agent manually refunded and re-placed the order. Looks like a frontend timeout issue.
resolution: Payments were queued during Stripe outage. No frontend bug. Charges authorized then completed. Users received delayed confirmation emails.

UNKNOWN incident: Order notifications delayed by hours
affected: orders
description: Push notifications for order status updates are arriving 3 to 4 hours late. Our notification service looks healthy. Could be an Apple or Google push service delay. Checking APNs status page now.
resolution: Root cause was Kafka consumer lag during incident. Notifications were queued and delivered in batch once lag cleared. APNs was not involved.

UNKNOWN incident: SSO integration returning 401 after maintenance
affected: auth
description: Enterprise SSO via SAML is broken. The SAML assertions are being rejected. IT admins think we changed our service provider metadata without telling them. Checking with the SSO team.
resolution: SAML metadata unchanged. Issue was JWT key rotation causing downstream 401s on session validation, not SAML. SSO team confirmed their config was correct.

UNKNOWN incident: Monitoring shows 100% error rate -- is prod down?
affected: gateway
description: PagerDuty alert: error rate at 100%. Checked Datadog, all red. First thought: bad Kubernetes rollout. Escalated to VP Engineering. Rolled back last deploy but error rate unchanged.
resolution: Rollback was unnecessary. Root cause was payments circuit breaker cascading to gateway, not a code issue. Unnecessary rollback introduced minor config regression.

P3 incident: Payments failing for American Express cards only
affected: payments
description: Visa and Mastercard work fine. Amex transactions failing with error code 1005. This has been happening for 2 days. Suspecting a Stripe Amex-specific configuration issue or a currency routing problem.
resolution: Amex transactions require a different API endpoint in our Stripe integration. A recent refactor accidentally hardcoded the Visa/MC endpoint for all card types.

UNKNOWN incident: Order history pagination broken -- shows same page repeatedly
affected: orders
description: Clicking next page in order history reloads the same first page. The pagination uses page number in the query param but the API is ignoring it and always returning page 1. Introduced in last sprint refactor.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Auth service not logging failed login attempts
affected: auth
description: Security audit finding: failed login attempts are not being written to the audit log. Successful logins are logged correctly. The logging call after authentication failure was accidentally removed in a cleanup PR.
resolution: Restored failed login audit logging. Backfill not possible for the 3-week gap. SIEM alert rules updated to also monitor rate-limiting events as a proxy signal.

UNKNOWN incident: Gateway rate limiter using wrong IP header
affected: gateway
description: The IP-based rate limiter reads from remote_addr instead of X-Real-IP. Since all traffic comes through the load balancer, every request appears to come from the same internal IP. Bot traffic is bypassing per-client rate limiting entirely.
resolution: Switched rate limiter to read X-Real-IP header. Per-IP limits now working correctly. This also explains why the earlier bot attack seemed to bypass rate limits.

P1 incident: Subscription renewal emails going to spam
affected: auth
description: Multiple subscribers reporting renewal reminder emails in spam folder. Email deliverability score is 42 out of 100. Our sending domain lacks DKIM and DMARC records. Also sending from a shared IP with low reputation.
resolution: Added DKIM records, published DMARC policy. Moved to dedicated sending IP and warmed it over 2 weeks. Deliverability score improved to 89 out of 100.

UNKNOWN incident: AWS bill $40k over budget this month
affected: gateway
description: AWS bill this month is $40,000 over the monthly budget. Finance flagged it. Initial investigation points to NAT gateway data transfer spike during the incident week. But EC2 cost also jumped unexpectedly.
resolution: NAT gateway spike caused by Fluentd log retry storms during incident sending huge volumes through NAT. EC2 cost jump was a dev environment left running. Both addressed.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders service not starting after node drain
affected: orders
description: After a Kubernetes node drain for maintenance, the orders-service pod failed to reschedule. Error: Insufficient memory. The pod requests 4GB but remaining nodes only have 3.8GB available. Resource request was bumped last sprint without checking cluster capacity.
resolution: Reduced orders-service memory request from 4GB to 2.5GB, actual usage is 1.8GB. Pod rescheduled immediately. Added cluster capacity check to pre-deploy pipeline.

UNKNOWN incident: Payments audit log missing transaction IDs
affected: payments
description: Compliance team flagged that 30% of entries in the payments audit log are missing the transaction_id field. This field is required for PCI DSS compliance. The field was made optional in a schema migration 6 weeks ago to fix a separate bug.
resolution: Made transaction_id required again in the audit log schema. Backfilled missing IDs from Stripe event logs where possible, 92% recovered. Flagged 8% gap to compliance team.

UNKNOWN incident: Wrong environment config loaded in production
affected: payments
description: The payments service in production is pointing to the staging Stripe API key. All production transactions are going through Stripe test mode and failing silently. Live cards are being rejected as invalid test cards.
resolution: Corrected environment variable. STRIPE_API_KEY_PROD was accidentally set to the test key in the production k8s secret. All transactions during the 40-minute window were re-attempted and succeeded.

P3 incident: OAuth callback URL mismatch after domain change
affected: auth
description: After migrating to a new domain, the Google OAuth callback URL was updated but Facebook and GitHub OAuth apps were not. Facebook and GitHub social login returning redirect_uri_mismatch error for all users.
resolution: Updated callback URLs in Facebook Developer Console and GitHub OAuth App settings. Tested all social login flows.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders export feature leaking other customer data
affected: orders
description: The bulk order export feature for admin users is including orders from ALL customers, not just the requesting account. There is no tenancy filter in the export query. Any admin-level user can download the entire customer order history.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Auth service rejecting valid MFA codes
affected: auth
description: Users with TOTP MFA enabled are having their valid codes rejected. The auth server clock drift is 45 seconds off NTP. TOTP has a 30-second tolerance window, so codes at the edge of validity are rejected.
resolution: Synced auth service hosts with NTP. Increased TOTP tolerance window to plus or minus 60 seconds. Drift was caused by a suspended VM that was resumed without clock sync.

P3 incident: Payments service generating duplicate invoice numbers
affected: payments
description: Invoice numbers are generated using a counter stored in Redis. After the Redis cluster failover last week, the counter reset to 1. We now have roughly 400 invoices with numbers that conflict with existing invoices from 6 months ago.
resolution: Implemented UUID-based invoice IDs instead of sequential counters. Renamed conflicting invoices with a -v2 suffix. Notified affected customers.

UNKNOWN incident: CPU spike when loading analytics dashboard
affected: orders
description: Loading the admin analytics dashboard spikes the orders DB CPU to 95% for 20 to 30 seconds. The dashboard fires 14 separate unoptimized queries simultaneously on page load. This was fine in dev with 1000 rows but production has 40 million orders.
resolution: Consolidated 14 queries into 3 optimized queries. Added materialized view for summary statistics refreshed every 5 minutes. Dashboard load time reduced from 28 seconds to 1.2 seconds.

UNKNOWN incident: Gateway not stripping internal headers before forwarding
affected: gateway
description: The gateway forwards X-Internal-User-Role headers sent by clients upstream to backend services. An attacker can set X-Internal-User-Role: admin in their request and backend services that trust this header will grant admin privileges.
resolution: Added header stripping for all X-Internal-* headers at the gateway before forwarding. Backend services now enforce authorization independently.

P1 incident: Payments retry logic retrying on non-retryable errors
affected: payments
description: The payment retry mechanism retries on all errors including validation errors such as invalid card number. A typo in a card number triggers 3 payment attempts and 3 declines, which can flag the customer as suspicious with their bank.
resolution: Updated retry logic to only retry on network errors and 429 or 503 status codes. Validation errors such as 400 and 402 are now returned immediately without retry.

P1 incident: Auth service bcrypt timing difference reveals valid usernames
affected: auth
description: Login timing varies depending on whether a username exists in the database. For valid usernames, bcrypt is run. For invalid usernames, we return immediately. This timing difference allows enumeration of valid user accounts.
resolution: open

P1 incident: Missing audit trail for admin actions
affected: auth
description: An internal security review found that admin-level actions such as refunds, account deletions, and plan changes are not recorded in the audit log. If an admin abuses their access or makes an error, there is no record of what was done or when.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: CORS errors blocking web app in Safari only
affected: gateway
description: Safari users getting CORS errors on API requests. Chrome and Firefox work fine. Safari is stricter about CORS preflight caching. Our gateway does not return the correct response to OPTIONS preflight requests for certain endpoints.
resolution: Fixed OPTIONS preflight handler to return correct Access-Control-Allow headers. Added CORS integration test covering Safari preflight behavior.

UNKNOWN incident: Orders microservice deployed without resource limits
affected: orders
description: New order-analytics sidecar was deployed without CPU or memory resource limits. It has been consuming unbounded CPU on shared nodes, causing resource starvation for neighboring pods. This explains the sporadic slowness on orders service this week.
resolution: Added resource requests and limits to analytics sidecar. Neighboring pods immediately stabilized. Made resource limits mandatory in deployment admission webhook.

UNKNOWN incident: Customer PII appearing in error tracking tool
affected: payments
description: Sentry error reports include full request payloads, which sometimes contain customer email addresses, names, and partial card numbers. This PII is being stored unencrypted in a third-party tool, which is a GDPR and PCI violation.
resolution: Added PII scrubbing middleware that redacts sensitive fields before sending to Sentry. Requested Sentry delete historical events via their data deletion API.

UNKNOWN incident: Slow order creation under high load -- mutex contention
affected: orders
description: Order creation P99 spikes to 12 seconds during flash sales. Profiling shows a global mutex in the order ID generation code being held for 200ms per request. Under load, requests queue up waiting for the lock.
resolution: Replaced sequential order ID generation with UUID v7. Mutex removed. P99 under same load dropped to 380ms.

UNKNOWN incident: Gateway health check slow response causing unhealthy marking
affected: gateway
description: ALB health check on the health endpoint returns 200 but takes 3.2 seconds because it runs a full DB query. The ALB marks instances as unhealthy after 2 seconds. Instances are being deregistered under load, reducing capacity and worsening the problem.
resolution: Changed health endpoint to return immediately with a shallow check. Added a separate internal deep-health endpoint. Instances re-registered within 2 minutes.

UNKNOWN incident: Inventory count going negative for high-demand SKUs
affected: orders
description: Three products show negative inventory counts. This means we oversold. The inventory reservation step runs asynchronously and there is a race condition when multiple orders arrive simultaneously for the same SKU. The check-and-decrement is not atomic.
resolution: open

UNKNOWN incident: Webhook delivery hammering a down merchant endpoint
affected: orders
description: Our webhook retry logic sends requests with exponential backoff but there is no maximum retry cap. One merchant endpoint has been down for 3 days and our system has made 15,000 retry attempts to it. The merchant is complaining about being DDoSed by our system.
resolution: Added maximum retry cap of 72 attempts over 72 hours. After cap, webhooks moved to dead-letter queue for manual review. Added merchant notification when their endpoint fails consistently.

UNKNOWN incident: On-call alert fatigue from false positive monitors
affected: auth
description: The on-call engineer received 340 PagerDuty alerts over the weekend, 90% false positives from flapping monitors. The auth service high-latency alert fires at over 200ms but normal P95 is 180 to 220ms. Thresholds were never recalibrated after infra migration.
resolution: Audited and recalibrated all alert thresholds based on 30-day baselines. Moved low-signal alerts to warning tickets instead of PagerDuty. Weekend alert volume reduced by 73%.

UNKNOWN incident: API v1 deprecated but still receiving 38% of traffic
affected: orders
description: API v1 was officially deprecated 6 months ago but still handling 38% of order traffic. Several enterprise customers never migrated. Shutting it down per the original plan would break their integrations. Migration deadline was communicated by email only.
resolution: open

P2 incident: Missing logs for 90-minute window during incident
affected: gateway
description: Post-incident review found application logs from 14:00 to 15:30 are missing from our log aggregation system. Fluentd was overwhelmed and dropped log records during peak error volume. This hampers post-mortem analysis.
resolution: Increased Fluentd buffer size and added backpressure handling. Enabled log persistence to local disk before forwarding to Loki. Added log ingestion rate to monitoring dashboards.

P1 incident: Auth service not invalidating tokens on password change
affected: auth
description: When a user changes their password, existing sessions remain valid for 24 hours. If an account is compromised and the user changes the password to kick out the attacker, the attacker token still works until natural expiry. This is a significant security gap discovered during a penetration test.
resolution: open
